challenge_summary: The architecture assessment presents a generally well-structured approach for a healthcare AI platform but contains four must-fix issues that could cause GDPR/NEN 7510 violations or deployment failure: (1) Cosmos DB partition key isolation is mischaracterized as 'cryptographic' when it is only logical, with no application-layer RLS mechanism described; (2) Log Analytics 30-day retention directly violates NEN 7510 and ISO 27001 Annex A.12.4 audit log requirements (minimum 1 year); (3) no technical component exists between hospital EHR systems and Event Hubs to enforce the 'anonymized references only' GDPR requirement; (4) GDPR-required DPIA and per-hospital DPA completion are prerequisites but are not modeled as hard gates blocking infrastructure deployment or data ingestion.
compact_for_parent: Pass 1 (security-governance) | HIGH | 4 must_fix, 5 should_fix | Key: [Cosmos DB partition key not cryptographic isolation]; [Log Analytics 30-day retention violates NEN 7510 audit log minimum]; [No PHI anonymization component before Event Hubs]

findings.title: Cosmos DB partition key isolation mischaracterized as cryptographic — PHI cross-tenant exposure risk
findings.recommendation: Remove 'cryptographic isolation' term. Document application-layer enforcement mechanism for hospital_id scoping. Consider separate Entra ID principals per hospital.

findings.title: Log Analytics 30-day retention violates NEN 7510 and ISO 27001 audit log requirements
findings.recommendation: Set retention to minimum 365 days. Add archive tier for 3-year access logs. Estimate: +€182/month for 1-year tier.

findings.title: No technical component enforces PHI anonymization before Event Hubs ingestion
findings.recommendation: Introduce ingestion proxy layer that validates payloads against PHI allowlist and pseudonymizes data before Event Hubs.

findings.title: GDPR DPIA and per-hospital DPA not modeled as hard deployment gates
findings.recommendation: Add DPIA gate (blocks prod deployment) and DPA gate (blocks per-hospital data ingestion) as architectural decisions.

findings.title: Cosmos DB Serverless backup conflicts with RPO=1 hour requirement
findings.recommendation: Switch to Provisioned Throughput with Continuous Backup (+€30-60/month) or document RPO exception.

findings.title: No Privileged Identity Management (PIM) for admin access — NEN 7510 violation
findings.recommendation: Implement Azure AD PIM for Owner/Contributor roles. Budget: ~€42/month for 7 engineers (Entra ID P2).

findings.title: Key Vault Standard tier blocks future CMK migration — requires destructive re-provisioning
findings.recommendation: Provision Key Vault Premium from day 1. Cost delta: ~€5-20/month. Enable soft-delete with purge protection.

findings.title: Defender for Cloud plans not enumerated — coverage gaps and budget underestimation
findings.recommendation: Enumerate required Defender plans (Storage, Key Vault, Containers). Estimate total: €100-200/month.

findings.title: EHR inbound API key authentication — no rotation, revocation, or attribution model
findings.recommendation: Use per-hospital APIM Subscription Keys with Key Vault rotation. Define 90-day rotation SLA and offboarding process.

findings.title: No certificate lifecycle management described for TLS endpoints
findings.recommendation: Use Key Vault-integrated certificates with auto-rotation. Add 30-day expiry alerts.

findings.title: Front Door WAF Prevention mode may block FHIR/HL7 healthcare payloads
findings.recommendation: Plan WAF tuning phase in Detection mode during initial onboarding before switching to Prevention.

findings.title: Governance policy discovery deferred — subscription Deny policies may block resources
findings.recommendation: Add Azure Policy risk to Top Architecture Risks table. Resolve in Step 3.5 before IaC coding.
